title: AI Engineer
description: Design, train, deploy, and maintain machine learning models and AI systems — from classical ML to large language models and generative AI.

lessons.title: Python Syntax, Data Types & Control Flow

quiz.question: Which data structure is ordered and mutable in Python?
quiz.explanation: Lists are ordered, mutable sequences in Python. Tuples are ordered but immutable.

projects.title: CLI Calculator with OOP
projects.description: Build a command-line calculator application using classes for different operations and a main interface class

quiz.question: What does 'self' represent in a Python class method?
options: The current instance of the class
quiz.explanation: 'self' refers to the current instance of the class, allowing access to instance attributes and methods.

projects.title: Data Cleaning Pipeline
projects.description: Use pandas to clean and transform a messy real-world dataset: handle missing values, standardize formats, and generate summary statistics

quiz.question: What is the primary advantage of NumPy arrays over Python lists for numerical computation?
options: They use contiguous memory and vectorized operations for faster computation
They can hold strings and numbers
quiz.explanation: NumPy arrays use contiguous memory blocks and vectorized operations implemented in C, making them significantly faster for numerical computations than Python lists.

quiz.question: What operation is fundamental to how neural networks transform inputs?
quiz.explanation: Matrix multiplication (dot product) is the core operation in neural network layers, transforming input vectors through learned weight matrices.

quiz.question: What does gradient descent optimize?
options: Model parameters by minimizing a loss function
quiz.explanation: Gradient descent iteratively adjusts model parameters in the direction that minimizes the loss function, using the gradient (direction of steepest ascent) to guide updates.

quiz.question: What does Bayes' theorem compute?
options: The probability of a hypothesis given observed evidence
quiz.explanation: Bayes' theorem calculates the posterior probability of a hypothesis based on prior knowledge and observed evidence: P(H|E) = P(E|H)*P(H)/P(E).

quiz.question: Which is an example of unsupervised learning?
quiz.explanation: K-means clustering is unsupervised because it groups data points based on similarity without using labeled training data.

projects.title: Housing Price Prediction
projects.description: Build a regression model to predict housing prices using linear regression, random forest, and XGBoost with proper cross-validation and hyperparameter tuning

quiz.question: What is the purpose of cross-validation?
options: To get a more reliable estimate of model performance on unseen data
quiz.explanation: Cross-validation splits data into multiple folds, training and evaluating on different subsets to provide a more robust estimate of how the model generalizes to unseen data.

quiz.question: What is one-hot encoding used for?
options: Converting categorical variables into binary columns
quiz.explanation: One-hot encoding creates binary (0/1) columns for each category, allowing ML algorithms to process categorical data numerically.

projects.title: EDA on Titanic Dataset
projects.description: Perform a full exploratory data analysis on the Titanic dataset with statistical summaries, distribution plots, correlation heatmaps, and feature relationship analysis

quiz.question: What chart type is best for showing the relationship between two continuous variables?
quiz.explanation: A scatter plot plots individual data points along two axes, making it ideal for revealing correlations and patterns between continuous variables.

quiz.question: What does an activation function introduce to a neural network?
options: Non-linearity so it can learn complex patterns
quiz.explanation: Activation functions (like ReLU, sigmoid, tanh) introduce non-linearity, allowing neural networks to learn complex, non-linear relationships in data.

projects.title: Image Classifier from Scratch
projects.description: Build a neural network in PyTorch to classify CIFAR-10 images, including data loading, training loop, and evaluation

quiz.question: What is the purpose of optimizer.zero_grad() in PyTorch?
options: To clear old gradients from the previous step
quiz.explanation: optimizer.zero_grad() resets the gradients of all parameters to zero before computing new gradients, preventing accumulation from previous steps.

projects.title: Build a CNN for Medical Image Classification
projects.description: Implement a CNN to classify chest X-ray images as normal or pneumonia using transfer learning with ResNet

quiz.question: What is the main advantage of CNNs over fully connected networks for images?
options: They use fewer parameters through weight sharing
They work on text data too
quiz.explanation: CNNs use shared filters (kernels) across spatial locations, drastically reducing parameters compared to fully connected networks while capturing spatial hierarchies.

resources.title: Stanford CS224n NLP with Deep Learning

projects.title: Time Series Prediction with LSTM
projects.description: Build an LSTM model in PyTorch to predict stock prices or weather patterns using sequential data

quiz.question: What problem do LSTMs solve that basic RNNs struggle with?
quiz.explanation: LSTMs use gating mechanisms (forget, input, output gates) to selectively remember or forget information, addressing the vanishing gradient problem in basic RNNs.

quiz.question: What is the main advantage of word embeddings over one-hot encoding?
options: They capture semantic relationships between words in a dense vector space
quiz.explanation: Word embeddings map words to dense vectors where similar words are close together, capturing semantic meaning (e.g., 'king' - 'man' + 'woman' ≈ 'queen').

projects.title: Build a Transformer from Scratch
projects.description: Implement a simplified transformer encoder in PyTorch with multi-head self-attention and positional encoding

quiz.question: What does self-attention allow a model to do?
options: Relate each token in a sequence to every other token
quiz.explanation: Self-attention computes attention weights between all pairs of tokens in a sequence, allowing the model to capture relationships regardless of distance.

resources.title: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners (GPT-3 Paper)

quiz.question: What is the key difference between GPT and BERT?
options: GPT is decoder-only (generative), BERT is encoder-only (bidirectional understanding)
quiz.explanation: GPT uses a decoder-only architecture trained for next-token prediction (generative), while BERT uses an encoder-only architecture trained with masked language modeling for bidirectional understanding.

projects.title: Fine-Tune a LLM on Custom Data
projects.description: Fine-tune a small open-source LLM (e.g., Llama 2 or Mistral) on a custom dataset using LoRA with the PEFT library

quiz.question: What is LoRA?
options: Low-Rank Adaptation - a parameter-efficient fine-tuning technique
A type of neural network architecture
quiz.explanation: LoRA freezes pre-trained model weights and injects trainable rank decomposition matrices, dramatically reducing the number of parameters to train.

projects.title: Build a RAG Chatbot
projects.description: Create a retrieval-augmented generation chatbot using LangChain, OpenAI embeddings, and a vector database (ChromaDB or Pinecone) that answers questions from a document collection

quiz.question: What is the main purpose of RAG?
options: To ground LLM responses in external, up-to-date knowledge
quiz.explanation: RAG retrieves relevant documents from an external knowledge base and includes them in the prompt, helping LLMs generate more accurate, factual, and up-to-date responses.

lessons.title: Deploying ML Models with FastAPI & Docker

projects.title: Deploy an ML Model as an API
projects.description: Package a trained ML model into a FastAPI service with Docker, including input validation, prediction endpoints, and health checks

quiz.question: Why is FastAPI preferred for ML model serving over Flask?
options: It has native async support, automatic docs, and Pydantic validation
quiz.explanation: FastAPI provides automatic OpenAPI docs, native async support, and Pydantic-based request validation, making it ideal for production ML APIs.

projects.title: Track Experiments with MLflow
projects.description: Set up MLflow to track experiments, log parameters/metrics, register model versions, and compare runs for an ML project

quiz.question: What does an MLflow Model Registry manage?
quiz.explanation: The MLflow Model Registry tracks model versions through their lifecycle stages (None → Staging → Production → Archived) with metadata and lineage.

projects.title: Build an ETL Pipeline
projects.description: Create an ETL pipeline that extracts data from multiple sources, validates it with Great Expectations, transforms it, and loads it into a feature store

quiz.question: What is the purpose of a feature store in ML?
options: To store precomputed features for training and serving consistency
quiz.explanation: A feature store centralizes feature computation, storage, and serving, ensuring consistency between training and production inference features.

projects.title: Transfer Learning for Medical Imaging
projects.description: Use a pretrained ResNet model to classify medical images (X-rays) with fine-tuning on a custom dataset

quiz.question: What is transfer learning?
options: Using a pretrained model as a starting point for a new task
quiz.explanation: Transfer learning leverages knowledge from a model pretrained on a large dataset and adapts it to a new, often smaller, dataset for a related task.

projects.title: Real-Time Object Detection App
projects.description: Build a real-time object detection application using YOLOv8 that processes webcam feed and identifies objects with bounding boxes and labels

quiz.question: What makes YOLO suitable for real-time detection?
options: It processes the entire image in a single forward pass
It only detects one object per image
quiz.explanation: YOLO (You Only Look Once) treats detection as a single regression problem, predicting bounding boxes and classes in one pass, making it extremely fast.

projects.title: Text-to-Image Generation Pipeline
projects.description: Build a text-to-image application using Stable Diffusion with the Hugging Face diffusers library, including prompt engineering and image post-processing

quiz.question: How do diffusion models generate images?
options: By gradually denoising random noise guided by learned patterns
By drawing pixels one at a time
quiz.explanation: Diffusion models learn to reverse a gradual noising process, starting from pure noise and iteratively denoising to generate new images conditioned on text or class labels.

projects.title: Train a GAN to Generate Faces
projects.description: Implement and train a DCGAN to generate human face images using the CelebA dataset with proper training stabilization techniques

quiz.question: In a GAN, what is the role of the discriminator?
options: Classify images as real or fake
quiz.explanation: The discriminator acts as a binary classifier that distinguishes real images from generated (fake) images, providing feedback to improve the generator.

projects.title: Optimize a Model for Edge Deployment
projects.description: Take a trained model, apply quantization and pruning, convert to ONNX format, and benchmark inference speed vs accuracy tradeoffs

quiz.question: What does INT8 quantization do to a model?
options: Reduces weight precision from 32-bit to 8-bit integers, making inference faster with small accuracy loss
quiz.explanation: Quantization reduces the numerical precision of model weights and activations (e.g., float32 to int8), decreasing model size and speeding up inference with typically minor accuracy degradation.

lessons.title: Cloud ML Platforms (AWS SageMaker, GCP Vertex AI)

projects.title: Train on Cloud GPU
projects.description: Set up and run a distributed training job on AWS SageMaker with GPU instances, including data upload, training script, and model export

quiz.question: What is a key benefit of using managed ML platforms like SageMaker?
options: They handle infrastructure provisioning, scaling, and monitoring for ML workflows
They replace the need for Python
quiz.explanation: Managed ML platforms abstract away infrastructure management, providing pre-built environments, auto-scaling, experiment tracking, and one-click deployment.

lessons.title: Build & Deploy an End-to-End ML System

projects.title: AI-Powered Application with RAG
projects.description: Build and deploy a production-ready AI application that combines RAG for knowledge retrieval, fine-tuned LLM for domain-specific responses, with a web UI, monitoring, and proper MLOps practices

projects.title: ML System Design Document
projects.description: Write a comprehensive ML system design document covering data pipelines, model architecture, training infrastructure, serving, monitoring, and failure modes

quiz.question: What should an ML monitoring system track in production?
options: Data drift, model performance metrics, latency, and error rates
Just the number of API calls
quiz.explanation: Production ML monitoring should track data distribution changes (drift), model prediction quality, serving latency, resource utilization, and error rates to detect degradation.

quiz.question: When designing an ML system, what should you consider FIRST?
options: The business problem and success metrics
quiz.explanation: Always start by understanding the business problem, defining success metrics, and clarifying constraints before selecting models or infrastructure.
